headline: 'We're Lucky Because We Have Our Own TPUs,' Says DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis — Yet Admits AI Memory Shortage Constrains Research
summary: The global artificial intelligence race is accelerating, but even the most advanced labs are running into hardware limits. Demis Hassabis, CEO of Google's DeepMind AI unit, recently told CNBC that shortages in memory, graphics processing units and electricity are slowing AI deployment. "Yes, I think that’s constraining a lot of the deployment for sure," Hassabis said. China Closing The Gap China remains central to the global AI competition.
